summary: Merged game-mode decompilation PR replacing three placeholder comments with matching C for fn_80170110, gm_801BEDA8, and fn_8017F09C. The functions cover unlock availability checks, CSS match-type cycling, and regular-clear time bonus calculation. Headers were updated to replace UNK prototypes with concrete void*, int, u8, and s32 signatures. The PR body and automated report both indicate 100% matches for the target gm functions.

key_files.role: Decompiled fn_80170110 unlock-table lookup and per-player unlock check.
Updated fn_80170110 and fn_801701C0 prototypes away from UNK_T/UNK_RET.
Decompiled gm_801BEDA8 CSS match_type cycling and VsModeData sync.
Decompiled fn_8017F09C clear time bonus eligibility and calculation.
Updated fn_8017F09C prototype to s32 fn_8017F09C(void).

what_changed: Implemented int fn_80170110(void* arg0, int idx, int mask, u8 player_id): reads kind from lbl_803D5A4C[idx], searches lbl_803D5A4C for the same kind, treats sentinel kind 0x29A as flags=0, checks ((u8) mask & flags), and routes standard kinds below 0xD7 to pl_80039418(player_id, kind) while other kinds call fn_801701C0(arg0, player_id, kind).
Changed gm_16F1.h prototypes: fn_80170110 now takes void*, int, int, u8, and fn_801701C0 is declared as int fn_801701C0(void*, int, int).

Implemented void gm_801BEDA8(MinorScene* arg0): obtains CSSData via gm_801A427C(arg0), obtains VsModeData via gm_801A5244(), decrements match_type with wrap to 0x17 when pending_scene_change == 2, otherwise increments match_type modulo 24, then copies css->data into *vs.

Implemented s32 fn_8017F09C(void): checks gm_8016AE38()->x24C8.x4_6, rejects cases involving lbl_80472D28.x118, lbl_80472D28.x11A/x11B, or x24C8.x0_7, then returns timer_seconds * lbl_80472D28.x108 clamped to nonnegative; otherwise returns 0.
Changed gmregclear.h prototype from UNK_RET fn_8017F09C(UNK_PARAMS) to s32 fn_8017F09C(void).
Verification in the PR body reported fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all submitted functions; the automated report listed fn_8017F09C, gm_801BEDA8, and fn_80170110 as new 100% matches.
smart_moves: Kept fn_80170110's table-search failure path as a shared check label with flags=0, avoiding duplicate return logic and matching the observed control-flow shape.
Used an explicit (u8) cast on mask before the unlock flag test, which is important for matching byte-width behavior around the flags field.
Expressed CSS match-type wrap in the natural domain range 0..23, using explicit reverse wrap to 0x17 and forward modulo 24 before syncing the full VS mode data struct.
Preserved the separate product and ret variables in fn_8017F09C before the negative clamp, a common tactic for matching signed arithmetic and branch shape.
Updated adjacent headers in the same PR so future decompilation work can rely on concrete function signatures instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
decomp_lessons: When a function first decompiles successfully, immediately replace UNK prototypes in headers if the argument and return types are clear from the matching implementation.
For table scans with a sentinel entry, model both the found and not-found paths explicitly; a goto to a shared post-scan check can be a legitimate matching tactic.
Bitfield-like struct accesses such as x24C8.x4_6 and x24C8.x0_7 should be preserved when field semantics are not yet known; premature renaming would not be evidence-grounded.
CSS state handlers often mutate a small field and then copy a larger embedded data struct into global mode settings; keep the copy ordering close to the original.
If a function calls an accessor twice in the matching C, as fn_8017F09C does with gm_8016AE38(), avoid folding it into one cached pointer unless matching has been rechecked.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-based names with subsystem prefixes: fn_80170110, gm_801BEDA8, fn_8017F09C, fn_801701C0.
Header declarations keep address comments such as /* 170110 */ and /* 17F09C */ next to symbols.
Global data types use lbl-address style names when semantics are still unclear: struct lbl_803D5A4C_t, struct lbl_80472D28_t, lbl_8046B6A0_t.
Known domain structs use descriptive names already present in the codebase: MinorScene, CSSData, VsModeData.
Unknown fields retain offset/bitfield-style names such as x4, x108, x118, x11A, x11B, x24C8.x4_6, and x24C8.x0_7.
The PR introduced a clearer parameter name player_id for fn_80170110's u8 player identifier.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Target functions were reported as 100.00% matches: fn_80170110 (+144 bytes), gm_801BEDA8 (+176 bytes), and fn_8017F09C (+176 bytes).
fn_80170110 uses a labeled check block after the lbl_803D5A4C scan to share the flag mask test between normal lookup and sentinel failure.
The unlock check uses constants 0x29A for the table sentinel/default path and 0xD7 as the split between pl_80039418 and fn_801701C0 handling.
gm_801BEDA8 uses an explicit decrement-or-wrap branch for reverse cycling but a modulo expression for forward cycling; this exact asymmetry is useful to try for CSS/menu wrap handlers.
fn_8017F09C keeps an explicit int flag variable, two gm_8016AE38() calls, and a product/ret clamp sequence, all of which may affect branch/register allocation for matching.
Automated GALE01 report showed overall matched code at 59.10% (+0.02%, +880 bytes), with some unrelated new matches and sdata2 regressions also reported by the matcher.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

Automated encounter report confirmed the three changed gm functions became 100.00% matches and noted two unmatched-item regressions in .sdata2, including main/melee/gm/gmregclear .sdata2 from 30.12% to 24.43%.
follow_up_queries: Find callers of fn_80170110 to determine a better type/name for arg0 and the meaning of idx and mask.
Investigate lbl_803D5A4C_t table entries and sentinel kind 0x29A to name kind and x4 fields more precisely.
Decompile or review fn_801701C0 to confirm the special unlock category handled when kind >= 0xD7.
Trace CSSData.match_type values 0..23 to name the match-type enum used by gm_801BEDA8.
Investigate lbl_80472D28.x108, x118, x11A, and x11B in gmregclear to name the clear bonus multiplier and eligibility flags.
Check whether the reported gmregclear .sdata2 regression needs separate cleanup or is only a matcher side effect.
